info.title: BRZ API
info.description:
Complete PIX gateway API by BRZ. Direct integration with BACEN — no external providers.

## Base URLs

**Base URL:** `https://api.brzip.com.br`

All endpoints use the base path `/api/v1/`.

## Authentication

The API uses two authentication methods:

### 1. Bearer Token (JWT) — Most endpoints

All endpoints **except Cash-In** use a Bearer Token:
``` Authorization: Bearer <your-jwt-token> ```
Obtain a JWT by calling `POST /api/v1/auth/bearer-token` with your API Key credentials.

### 2. API Key — Cash-In endpoints only

Cash-In endpoints (`/api/v1/pix/cash-in/*`) use two headers:
``` x-key-id: <your-key-id> x-secret-key: <your-secret-key> ```


## Rate Limits

- Default: 50 req/s per client, burst up to 100.
- When exceeded, returns HTTP 429 with `Retry-After` header.

## Common Headers

| Header | Required | Description | |--------|----------|-------------| | `Authorization` | Yes (Bearer) | `Bearer <jwt>` for PIX endpoints | | `x-key-id` | Yes (Cash-In) | API Key ID | | `x-secret-key` | Yes (Cash-In) | API Key Secret | | `X-Idempotency-Key` | Recommended | Prevents duplicate operations (UUID) | | `Content-Type` | Yes (POST/PUT) | Always `application/json` |

---

## Onboarding Flow (Step by Step)

### Step 1 — Create Account

Create your account with `POST /api/v1/auth/signup`. Save the returned `keyId` and `secretKey`. OTP codes will be sent via email and SMS.

### Step 1b — Verify Email

Verify your email with `POST /api/v1/auth/verify-email` using the OTP code received.

### Step 1c — Verify Phone

Verify your phone with `POST /api/v1/auth/verify-phone` using the OTP code received via SMS.

### Step 1d — Resend OTP (optional)

If needed, resend the code with `POST /api/v1/auth/resend-otp` (max 5 per hour).

### Step 2 — Login

Log in with `POST /api/v1/auth/login` to obtain a session token.

### Step 3 — Submit Documents (3 stages)

1. Get pre-signed URLs with `POST /api/v1/auth/documents/presigned-urls`
2. `PUT` each file directly to the S3 URL (raw binary, no FormData)
3. Confirm uploads with `POST /api/v1/auth/documents/confirm`

**Individual documents:** `cpf_frente`, `cpf_verso`, `selfie_com_cpf`, `comprovante_residencia`
**Business documents:** `cnpj`, `contrato_social`, `comprovante_residencia_empresa`

### Step 4 — Wait for Approval

Track your status with `GET /api/v1/auth/status`. Wait for team approval.

### Step 5 — Generate Bearer Token

After approval, generate your Bearer Token with `POST /api/v1/auth/bearer-token` using your credentials.

### Step 6 — Register PIX Key

Register a PIX key in DICT/BACEN with `POST /api/v1/pix/keys`. Just send `{"key_type": "EVP"}` — account data is automatically populated from your onboarding!

### Step 7 — Ready to Operate!

Now you can create charges (Cash-In), make transfers, and check your balance.

---

servers.description: PIX QR Code Server (public, no auth)

pix_key.description: PIX key used in the transaction

e2e.description: End-to-end ID assigned by the Central Bank

channel.description: Channel through which the transaction was initiated

processed_at.description: Timestamp when the transaction was processed

created_at.description: Timestamp when the transaction was created

ledger_entries.description: Ledger entries showing balance movements for this transaction

registered.description: Indicates if the key was successfully registered

keyStatus.description: Status of the key in DICT

success.description: Indicates if the key was successfully deleted

correlation_id.description: Request correlation ID for support tracing

from_cache.description: Indicates if the balance was served from cache

wallet_breakdown.description: Per-wallet breakdown (present when client holds multiple wallets)

bearerAuth.description: JWT token obtained via `POST /api/v1/auth/bearer-token`. Required for all endpoints except Cash-In (which uses API Key headers).

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
Generate and manage authentication tokens for the BRZ API.

Use `POST /api/v1/auth/bearer-token` with your API Key credentials to obtain a JWT token required for most API endpoints.

tags.name: Service Health
tags.description: Health check endpoints to verify service availability. No authentication required.

tags.name: PIX Keys
tags.description:
Manage PIX keys registered in the BACEN DICT (Directory of Transactional Account Identifiers).

**Authentication:** Bearer Token
**Required scopes:** `pix:keys:read` and/or `pix:keys:write`

tags.name: PIX Balance
tags.description:
Query account balance information.

**Authentication:** Bearer Token
**Scope:** `pix:balance:read`

tags.name: Transactions
tags.description:
Query and retrieve PIX transaction details for the authenticated client. Supports filtering by type, status, end-to-end ID, reference, and date range.

**Authentication:** Bearer Token

**Scope:** Bearer JWT obtained via `/api/v1/auth/bearer-token`

**Date Range Filtering:** Use `date_from` and `date_to` parameters (RFC 3339 format). The range cannot exceed 30 days — the API returns HTTP 400 if exceeded.

tags.name: PIX Limits
tags.description:
Query and manage PIX transaction limits.

**Authentication:** Bearer Token

**Scope:** `pix:limits:read` / `pix:limits:write`

**Important:** Approved limits that are not consistently used will be automatically downgraded. If your limit is reduced due to inactivity, you will need to submit a new limit increase request.

tags.name: PIX Cash-In
tags.description:
Create and manage PIX charges (Cash-In). Generates a QR Code for the payer.

**Authentication:** API Key via headers `x-key-id` + `x-secret-key`, OR Bearer Token.

**Endpoints:**
- `POST /api/v1/pix/cash-in/qrcode` — Create charge
- `GET /api/v1/pix/cash-in/{id}` — Query status
- `POST /api/v1/pix/cash-in/{id}/cancel` — Cancel charge

**Financial fields (CobV — charge with due date):**
- `due_date` — Due date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `penalty` — Late fee after due date (`mode` + `value`)
- `interest` — Daily interest (`mode` + `value`)
- `discount` — Early payment discount (`mode` + `value` + `fixed_dates[]`)
- `rebate` — Rebate/abatement (`mode` + `value`)

tags.name: PIX Reversals
tags.description:
Manage reversals (refunds) of received PIX payments.

**Authentication:** Bearer Token

**Required scopes:**
- `pix:reversals:write` to create reversals
- `pix:reversals:read` to query reversals

tags.name: PIX Cash-Out
tags.description:
Send PIX payments (Cash-Out) via Temporal workflows.

**Authentication:** Bearer Token
**Scope:** `pix:cashout:write` / `pix:cashout:read`

tags.name: MED
tags.description:
Respond to inbound MED (Special Return Mechanism) requests and list them by status. MED is a BACEN mechanism for returning PIX funds in cases of fraud or operational errors.

**Authentication:** Bearer Token
**Scope:** `pix:med:read` / `pix:med:write`

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description:
Register URLs to receive real-time HTTP POST notifications when events occur in your account — PIX payments, MED requests, onboarding updates, and more.

**Scopes:** `webhooks:read` (list, search) · `webhooks:write` (create, update, delete, resend)

For the full integration guide including supported events, payload examples, signature verification and retry behaviour, see the [Webhooks Integration guide](/guides/webhooks-integration).

tags.name: P2P
tags.description:
Look up internal accounts and execute peer to peer (P2P) transfers.
**Authentication:** Bearer token
**Scope:** `pix:p2p:read` / `pix:p2p:write`

tags.name: Accounts
tags.description:
Manage account state transitions — activate, block, or reserve accounts.

These endpoints are used to manage the lifecycle of a payment account after it has been created through the onboarding flow. Accounts start in `pending_kyc` state and must be activated before they can transact.

**Account states:** `pending_kyc → active → suspended | blocked | closed`

**Authentication:** Bearer token

tags.name: Boleto
tags.description:
Decode barcode data and make boleto payments using the BRZ API.

Boleto processing is handled through the payment orchestrator and settled via the Celcoin integration. Supports both boleto payments (paying a third-party bill) and boleto issuance (charging your customers).

**Authentication:** Bearer token

**Idempotency:** Always include `X-Idempotency-Key` when creating payments.

tags.name: Wallets
tags.description:
Query wallet balances and validate funds availability.

The Wallet API (`v1-1-beta`) provides real-time balance information with cache invalidation driven by ledger events. The `wallet_breakdown` field shows a per-wallet breakdown when a client holds multiple wallets.

> **Note:** The `v1-1-beta` path prefix indicates this contract is stable but may evolve. Plan for a future migration to `/api/v1/wallet/*`.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

tags.name: Participants
tags.description:
Query the list of financial institutions participating in the PIX network (STR/COMPE directory).

Useful for validating ISPBs, looking up bank names, and checking PIX participation status before initiating transfers.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

tags.name: PIX QR Code Server
tags.description:
Public endpoints served by the BRZ QR Code server at `qrcode.brzip.com.br`.

These endpoints resolve PIX QR code access tokens to signed JWS payloads (RFC 7515) containing the full EMV QRCODE data. No authentication is required — security is enforced through opaque access tokens and expiration.

**Base URL:** `https://qrcode.brzip.com.br`
**Authentication:** None (public endpoints)

The response is a compact JWS (`application/jose`) with three base64url-encoded parts (`header.payload.signature`). The payload contains PIX-standard fields: `chave`, `valor`, `devedor`, `recebedor`, `calendario`, and `solicitacaoPagador`.

The JWKS endpoint (`/pix/jwks`) exposes the public key for signature verification, compliant with BACEN PIX spec.

post.description:
Generate a JWT Bearer Token using your API Key credentials.

**Authentication:** Pass your credentials via headers `x-key-id` and `x-secret-key`.

**Request body:** Optionally specify the `scopes` array to limit the token's permissions. If omitted, the token includes all scopes available to your API Key.

The returned token should be included in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <token>` for all subsequent requests.

parameters.name: x-key-id
parameters.description: Your API Key ID, provided after account approval.

parameters.name: x-secret-key
parameters.description: Your API Secret Key, provided after account approval.

scopes.description: List of scopes to include in the token. If omitted, all scopes available to the API Key are included.

token.description: JWT Bearer Token to use in the Authorization header.

scopes.description: List of scopes included in the token.

refreshToken.description: Token to refresh the Bearer Token when it expires.

get.description:
Basic service health check. Returns HTTP 200 if the service is running.

**Response:** `{"status":"healthy","service":"brz-api"}`

Checks if the service is ready to accept requests. Returns HTTP 200 when ready.

**Response:** `{"status":"ready","service":"brz-api"}`

post.description:
Resolves a PIX key against the BACEN DICT database, returning the linked account's bank details and owner information.
Use this to display recipient information before initiating a Cash-Out, or to validate that a key exists before attempting a transfer.
**Scope:** `pix:lookup`
**Authentication:** Bearer token or API Key

end_to_end_id.description: E2E ID to use as `X-Pix-Lookup-E2E` header in Cash-Out

404.description: PIX key not found in DICT

post.description:
Registers a new PIX key in DICT/BACEN.

**Simplified!** Only `key_type` is required. Account data (account_type, account_number, branch_number, person_type, tax_id, name, trade_name, account_open_date) is automatically populated from the approved onboarding data.

**Key types (`key_type`):**
- `CPF` — Individual taxpayer ID (11 digits)
- `CNPJ` — Business taxpayer ID (14 digits)
- `EMAIL` — Email address
- `PHONE` — Phone number (+55...)
- `EVP` — Random key (generated by BACEN, send empty `key`)

**Required field:** `key_type`
**Optional field:** `key` (required for CPF, CNPJ, EMAIL, PHONE; empty for EVP)

**Optional override fields:** To override onboarding data, send any of: `account_type`, `account_number`, `branch_number`, `person_type`, `tax_id`, `name`, `trade_name`, `account_open_date`

**Tip:** For EVP (random key), just send `{"key_type": "EVP"}`. BACEN generates the key automatically.

**Scope:** `pix:keys:write`

key_type.description: Type of PIX key to register

key.description: Key value. Required for CPF, CNPJ, EMAIL, PHONE. Leave empty for EVP.

success.description: Indicates if the operation was successful

get.description:
Lists all PIX keys registered for the authenticated client.

**Scope:** `pix:keys:read`

Looks up a specific PIX key by its value (CPF, email, EVP UUID, etc.).

**Scope:** `pix:keys:read`

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The PIX key value to look up

delete.description:
Removes a PIX key from DICT/BACEN.

**URL parameter:** `key` — the PIX key value
**Body:** `reason` (optional, defaults to `USER_REQUESTED`)

**Scope:** `pix:keys:write`

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The PIX key value to delete

get.description:
Queries the account balance. If `account_id` is omitted, uses the client's default account.

**Scope:** `pix:balance:read`

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Account ID (optional, uses default account if omitted)

get.description:
Lists all transactions for the authenticated client with pagination and filtering by type, status, end-to-end ID, reference, and date range.

**Scope:** Bearer JWT

**Date Range:** When using `date_from` and `date_to`, the range cannot exceed 30 days. Returns HTTP 400 if exceeded.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of records per page (1-100, default 20)

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Start of date range filter (RFC 3339 format). Must be used together with `date_to`. Range cannot exceed 30 days.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: End of date range filter (RFC 3339 format). Must be used together with `date_from`. Range cannot exceed 30 days.

400.description: Bad request — e.g., date range exceeds 30 days

get.description:
Retrieves full details of a specific transaction by ID, including payer/receiver information, bank details, and ledger entries. Returns 404 if the transaction does not belong to the authenticated client.

**Scope:** Bearer JWT

Queries PIX transaction limits for the account, grouped by type (pix_out, pix_in, ted, boleto) and period (daytime/nighttime).

**Scope:** `pix:limits:read`

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Account ID (optional, uses default account if omitted)

limits.description: Limits grouped by type and period

post.description:
Creates a request to change a PIX transaction limit. The request remains pending until approved or rejected by an administrator.

**Rules:**
- Only one pending request per type/period is allowed
- If one already exists, returns HTTP 409

**Scope:** `pix:limits:write`

409.description: A pending request already exists for this type/period

get.description:
Lists all pending limit change requests for the authenticated account. Returns only requests with `pending` status.

**Scope:** `pix:limits:read`

Retrieves details of a specific limit change request. Returns full data including current status (pending, approved, rejected, expired).

**Scope:** `pix:limits:read`

post.description:
Creates a PIX charge (Cash-In). Generates a QR Code for the payer and waits for the complete processing result.

**Authentication:** API Key via headers `x-key-id` + `x-secret-key`, OR Bearer Token.

**Scope:** `pix:cashin:write`

parameters.name: x-key-id
parameters.description: API Key ID (required if not using Bearer Token)

parameters.name: x-secret-key
parameters.description: API Key Secret (required if not using Bearer Token)

get.description:
Queries the status of a PIX charge.

**Possible statuses:** `pending`, `paid`, `expired`, `cancelled`, `failed`

**Scope:** `pix:cashin:read`

post.description:
Cancels a pending PIX charge. Only works for charges with `pending` status.

**Scope:** `pix:cashin:write`

400.description: Cash-In cannot be cancelled (not in pending status)

post.description:
Creates a reversal (refund) of a received PIX payment.

**Scope:** `pix:reversals:write`

amount.description: Amount in BRL to refund (e.g. "10.00" = R$10.00)

external_id.description: Unique reversal ID in your system

end_to_end_id.description: EndToEndID of the original received PIX payment

get.description:
Lists all PIX reversals with pagination and filter support. This endpoint is not implemented as of yet. Use [GET /reversals/:reversal_id](api:GET/api/v1/pix/reversals/{reversal_id}) to retrieve a specific reversal.

**Scope:** `pix:reversals:read`

Retrieves the status and details of a PIX reversal by its ID.

**Scope:** `pix:reversals:read`

post.description:
Initiates a PIX payment (Cash-Out). This single endpoint supports three different payment flows depending on the fields provided:

## Flow 1 — Via PIX Key

Send a payment directly using the recipient's PIX key.
**Required fields:** `amount`, `pix_key`, `pix_key_type`, `external_id`

## Flow 2 — Via Bank Details

Send a payment using the recipient's bank account details, without needing a PIX key.
**Required fields:** `amount`, `receiver_name`, `receiver_document`, `receiver_ispb`, `receiver_branch`, `receiver_account`, `receiver_account_type`, `external_id`

## Flow 3 — Via QR Code (Two-Step)

1. First decode the QR Code via `POST /api/v1/pix/qrcode/decode`
2. Use the returned `end_to_end_id` as the `X-Pix-Lookup-E2E` header in this call
3. The system retrieves pre-decoded data and processes the payment (without decoding again)

**Required fields:** `amount` + header `X-Pix-Lookup-E2E`

**Scope:** `pix:cashout:write`

external_id.description: Unique cash-out ID in your system (used for idempotency and status lookup)

receiver_document.description: Recipient CPF (11 digits) or CNPJ (14 digits)

receiver_ispb.description: Recipient bank ISPB (optional — resolved from PIX key if omitted)

br_code.description: Raw BR Code from a decoded QR (optional — use instead of pix_key when paying via QR)

parameters.name: X-Pix-Lookup-E2E
parameters.description: EndToEndID from a previous QR Code decode (required for Flow 3). Obtained from `POST /api/v1/pix/qrcode/decode`.

post.description:
Returns a paginated list of Cash-Out transactions matching the given filters.
**Scope:** `pix:cashout:read`

get.description:
Queries the status of a PIX payment (Cash-Out).

**Possible statuses:** `processing`, `completed`, `failed`, `returned`

**Scope:** `pix:cashout:read`

post.description:
Decodes a PIX QR Code (static or dynamic).

**Required field:** `br_code` (EMV payload from QR Code)

**Participant fields (resolved via BACEN ParticipantesSTR list):**
- `receiver_participant_name` — Recipient institution name (always present)
**Financial fields (returned when present in CobV QR):**
- `due_date` — Due date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `penalty` — Late fee: `mode` (1=fixed, 2=%), `value`
- `interest` — Interest: `mode` (1=BRL/day, 2=%/day, 3=%/month, 4=%/year), `value`
- `discount` — Discount: `mode` (1-4), `value`, `fixed_dates[]`
- `rebate` — Rebate: `mode` (1=fixed, 2=%), `value`
- `final_amount` — Final amount calculated by BACEN (with charges applied)
**Optimized flow (Two-Step — RECOMMENDED):**
1. Decode the QR Code with this API (data is saved in lookup history)
2. Confirm recipient details with the user
3. Send the Cash-Out using the `X-Pix-Lookup-E2E: {end_to_end_id}` header (WITHOUT sending `br_code` again)
4. The system uses pre-decoded data, avoiding a duplicate decode call

**Scope:** `pix:cashout:write`

br_code.description: EMV payload from the QR Code

end_to_end_id.description: Use this in X-Pix-Lookup-E2E header for the two-step flow

get.description: Lists all MED requests for the authenticated client. **Scope:** `pix:med:read`

post.description:
Opens a new MED (Mecanismo Especial de Devolução — Special Return Mechanism) request to recover funds from a fraudulent PIX transaction.

Use this when your client was a victim of fraud and the original transaction was received (cash-in) by the alleged fraudster. BACEN will place a precautionary hold on the recipient's account while the MED is under analysis.

**Scope:** `pix:med:write`

end_to_end_id.description: End-to-end ID of the original PIX transaction to be refunded

amount.description: Amount to recover in BRL (must not exceed the original transaction amount)

refund_reason.description: BACEN refund reason code. Common values: `1` = Fraud, `2` = Operational error, `3` = Customer request. See BACEN MED documentation for full list.

direction.description: Direction of the refund from your perspective: `DEBIT` = you are requesting the refund (payer/victim side); `CREDIT` = you received a refund request (payee/suspect side).

details.description: Free-text details about the reason for the MED request
details.example: Cliente relata estelionato via engenharia social

client_id.description: Client ID on whose behalf to open the MED (optional — defaults to authenticated client)

400.description: Invalid request or end_to_end_id not found

409.description: MED already exists for this end_to_end_id

get.description: Returns details of a single MED request. **Scope:** `pix:med:read`

post.description: Cancels an open MED request. Only possible while status is `CREATED`. **Scope:** `pix:med:write`

reason.example: Resolved directly with the other party

409.description: MED cannot be cancelled in current state

post.description:
Responds to an inbound MED (Special Return Mechanism) request.

**Possible actions:**
- `approve` — Approves the return (captures the precautionary hold and credits the funds)
- `reject` — Rejects the return (releases the precautionary hold)

**Scope:** `pix:med:write`

action.description: Action to take on the MED request

get.description:
Lists MED requests filtered by status.

**Possible statuses:** `OPEN`, `IN_ANALYSIS`, `APPROVED`, `REJECTED`, `CLOSED`

**Scope:** `pix:med:read`

post.description:
Creates a new webhook to receive PIX event notifications.

**IMPORTANT:** The `secret` is returned **only at creation time**. Store it securely for signature validation.

url.description: HTTPS URL that will receive notifications

events.description: List of events to subscribe to. If empty or `["*"]`, receives all events.

get.description: Lists all webhooks registered by the authenticated client.
Retrieves a specific webhook by ID. Returns webhook details including status and last activity timestamps.

put.description: Updates an existing webhook. All fields are optional — send only what you want to change.

delete.description:
Removes a registered webhook. Pending deliveries for this webhook will be cancelled.

**WARNING:** This action is irreversible.

get.description: Lists webhook deliveries for the client. Each delivery shows the event, status, payload sent, response received, attempt count, and timestamps.
Retrieves details of a specific delivery, including the payload sent, HTTP response status, and number of attempts.

post.description:
Manually resends a failed or undelivered webhook delivery.

Useful when:
- Your URL was down and is now back online
- You missed a notification and need to reprocess
- You want to test integration with real data

The delivery will be re-queued and delivered on the next worker execution.

Create a P2P transfer
**Scope:** `pix:p2p:write`

receiver_account_id.description: Account id of the receiver. Obtained from field `account_id` of the [lookup](api:POST/api/v1/pix/p2p/lookup) flow

error.example: Receiver PIX key not found internally

post.description:
Looks up a user's account by their PIX key or account number
**Scope:** `pix:p2p:read`

error.example: search_type must be one of: email, phone, document, account

post.description:
Transition an account from `pending_kyc` to `active` state after KYC approval. Only accounts in `pending_kyc` state can be activated.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

changed_by.description: Identifier of the operator or system triggering the activation

correlation_id.description: Optional correlation ID for audit trail

post.description:
Block an account, preventing all financial operations. A blocked account can be suspended or closed but cannot be reactivated directly.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

changed_by.description: Identifier of the operator or system triggering the block

correlation_id.description: Optional correlation ID for audit trail

400.description: Invalid state transition or missing reason

post.description:
Reserves an account number slot before the full account is created. Call this during onboarding to obtain a `reservation_id` and `account_number` that you pass later to `POST /api/v1/accounts`.

**Required header:** `X-Client-ID` (the client for whom to reserve)

**Authentication:** Bearer token

parameters.name: X-Client-ID
parameters.description: Client ID for whom to reserve the account number

account_number.description: Specific account number to reserve (optional — auto-assigned if omitted)

reservation_id.description: Reservation token — pass this to POST /api/v1/accounts

branch_code.description: Branch code associated with the reservation

400.description: Invalid request or missing X-Client-ID header

409.description: Account number already reserved or in use

get.description: Returns the account associated with a given client ID. **Authentication:** Bearer token

post.description:
Temporarily suspends an account, restricting financial operations. Unlike `block`, a suspended account may be reactivated.
**Authentication:** Bearer token

changed_by.description: Identifier of the operator triggering the suspension

get.description: Returns the full state transition history for an account. **Authentication:** Bearer token
Returns all wallets for the authenticated client with balances. **Authentication:** Bearer token

post.description:
Decode a boleto barcode (linha digitável or código de barras) and return structured payment details including the amount, due date, and beneficiary information.

Use this endpoint before creating a payment to confirm the bill details with your user.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

barcode.description: Boleto barcode (linha digitável or código de barras, with or without dots/spaces)

422.description: Barcode valid but payment not allowed (e.g., expired)

post.description:
Initiate a boleto payment. The payment is processed asynchronously — use `GET /api/v1/payments/boleto/{paymentId}` to track the status.

Always include `X-Idempotency-Key` to prevent duplicate payments. The key is scoped to your client and expires after 24 hours.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

parameters.name: X-Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Unique key to prevent duplicate payments (recommended)

amount.description: Payment amount (must match or override boleto amount for flex boletos)

scheduled_date.description: Optional scheduled payment date (ISO 8601, must be >= today and <= due_date)

422.description: Payment not allowed (insufficient funds, expired boleto, etc.)

get.description:
Retrieve the current status and details of a boleto payment.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

delete.description:
Cancel a pending boleto payment. Only payments in `pending` status can be cancelled. Payments already in `processing` or `completed` cannot be reversed via this endpoint.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

400.description: Cannot cancel a payment in current status

get.description:
Retrieve the real-time wallet balance for a client. The response includes `available`, `blocked`, and `total` amounts, plus a `wallet_breakdown` array when the client holds multiple wallets.

Balance is cached (default TTL: 5 minutes) and invalidated by ledger events. Use the `from_cache` field to detect stale reads.

**Path prefix:** `/api/v1-1-beta/` — stable beta contract.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

post.description:
Retrieve wallet balances for multiple clients in a single request. Useful for dashboards and reconciliation flows. Maximum 100 client IDs per request.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

400.description: Empty list or list exceeds 100 items

get.description:
Search the list of financial institutions participating in the PIX network. Results include ISPB, name, short name, and participation flags.

**Authentication:** Bearer token

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query — filters by bank name, short name, or ISPB

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

get.description:
Resolve a PIX QR code access token to a signed JWS payload (RFC 7515).

**Base URL:** `https://qrcode.brzip.com.br`
**Authentication:** None — this is a public endpoint.

The response `Content-Type` is `application/jose`. The JWS compact serialization format is `<header>.<payload>.<signature>` (three base64url parts joined by dots). Decode each part with standard base64url decoding to inspect the contents.

The payload contains PIX EMV data: `chave`, `valor`, `devedor`, `recebedor`, `calendario`, and `solicitacaoPagador`.

To verify the signature, fetch the JWKS from `GET /pix/jwks`.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Opaque access token — UUID or short code provided in the QR code URL

404.description: Access token not found or expired

get.description:
Resolve a dynamic PIX QR code (CobV) with real-time recalculation of penalties, interest, and discounts according to BACEN PIX spec.

**Base URL:** `https://qrcode.brzip.com.br`
**Authentication:** None — this is a public endpoint.

Unlike static QR codes, each GET recalculates `valor.final`, `valor.multa`, `valor.juros`, and updates `calendario.apresentacao` with the current timestamp. The response is always a fresh signed JWS.

200.description: Dynamic JWS payload with recalculated amounts

schema.description: Compact JWS with updated CobV payload

404.description: Access token not found or expired

get.description:
Returns the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) containing the public key(s) used to sign QR code JWS payloads. Use these keys to verify signatures on `/pix/qr/{accessToken}` and `/pix/cobv/{accessToken}` responses.

**Base URL:** `https://qrcode.brzip.com.br`
**Authentication:** None — this is a public endpoint.

Required by BACEN PIX spec for JWS signature validation. The JWKS includes `alg`, `kid`, and optionally `x5c` and `x5t#S256`.
